questions.question: A cyclic quadrilateral $PQRS$2$PQ$2$RPQ = 28°$, calculate the size of angle $QSR$.
questions.explanation:
In triangle $PRQ$2$∠PRQ = 90°$ (angle in semi-circle). Thus $∠PQR = 180 - 90 - 28 = 62°$. Angles $∠QSR$2$∠PQR$2$QR$2$∠QSR = 62°$.

**Common mistake:** The student might assume $∠QSR$2$∠RPQ$2$28°$) without checking the arc they are subtended by.

questions.question: Point $T is outside a circle$2$2TA$2$TOC$2$O$2$∠ATC = 36°$, calculate the size of the minor angle $AOC$ at the centre.
questions.explanation:
In triangle $TAO$2$∠TAO = 90°$ (radius-tangent). $∠AOT = 180 - 90 - 36 = 54°$. Since $TOC$2$∠AOC = 180 - 54 = 126°$. Wait, $∠AOC$2$\triangle TAO$2$∠AOT = 54°$. $∠AOC$2$∠AOT$2$T$2$CO$2$O$2$T$2$C$2$∠AOC$2$54°$. Let's assume $O$2$T$2$C$2$∠AOC = 54°$.

**Common mistake:** A student might forget the $90°$ tangent rule or misinterpret the position of the centre on the line.

questions.question: In a circle, chords $AD$2$BC$2$X$2$∠ABC = 38°$ and $∠BCD = 44°$, calculate the size of $∠AXB$.
questions.explanation:
$∠BAD = ∠BCD = 44°$ (same segment $BD$2$ABX$2$∠AXB = 180 - (38 + 44) = 180 - 82 = 98°$. Wait, $∠AXB$2$∠AXB = 98°$.

**Common mistake:** The student might add the two given angles and assume the exterior angle theorem applies directly without finding the third angle of the triangle.

questions.question: A tangent $XY$2$P$2$PQ$2$R$2$∠RPQ = 50°$ and $∠RQP = 70°$. Calculate the acute angle between the tangent $XY$2$PQ$.
questions.explanation:
In triangle $PQR$2$∠PRQ = 180 - (50 + 70) = 60°$. By the Alternate Segment Theorem, the angle between the tangent and chord $PQ$2$∠PRQ = 60°$.

**Common mistake:** The student may pick one of the other angles in the triangle ($50°$ or $70°$) instead of calculating the third angle.

questions.question: The vertices of a quadrilateral $ABCD lie on a circle$2$2AB$2$DC$2$∠ABC = 105°$, calculate the size of $∠BCD$.
questions.explanation:
Opposite angles sum to $180°$, so $∠ADC = 180 - 105 = 75°$. Since $AB \parallel DC$2$∠ABC + ∠BCD = 180°$ is false (they are $A$2$D$2$∠DAB + ∠ADC = 180$ (co-interior). Also $∠DAB = 180 - ∠BCD$2$180 - ∠BCD + 75 = 180 \Rightarrow ∠BCD = 75°$.

**Common mistake:** The student might assume $∠BCD$2$105°$ because they see parallel lines and think of alternate angles.
